requiredEvidence.evidence: The independent repository contains the Tools package, its release infrastructure, and a fresh lockfile.

The exact pi-leetcode-tools@0.1.4 candidate passed typecheck, 29 files / 283 tests, reproducible build, 24/24 upstream completeness and adapter fixtures, source/packed 344/344, packed install, 14-Tool Pi activation, local publish-path regression, a 162-file secret scan with zero findings, and supply-chain verification on Node 24.18.0.

GitHub Actions main push run 29713477233 and immutable-tag run 29713609886 passed the exact 0.1.4 release pipeline on Windows and Ubuntu with Node 22.19.0 and Node 24. Both runs rebuilt and verified CandidateRecord 09e4524e..., the 323586-byte tgz, production audit, packed install, 14-Tool Pi activation and supply-chain evidence.

Both protected workflows publish explicit ./ local tgz paths. The static gate validates bootstrap and regular publish jobs, includes 14 negative checks, and a real npm --dry-run regression passed for the downloaded bootstrap-bundle and release-bundle layouts.

Version 0.1.4 resolves installed Gateway durable state through Pi's standard agent directory when PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR is absent, projects cpa-facing combined root schemas into plain objects while preserving complete Gateway validation, and adds packed ready-event and real Pi activation regressions. The runtime fix passed CI run 29712517900, a Pi 0.80.10 plus cpa probe returned OK, and the exact 0.1.4 tgz activated all 14 tools. Contract 1.1.0, protocol 1.0.0, schema/behavior/capability/resource digests, the 24-interface upstream surface, and regional adapter semantics remain unchanged.

The separately authorized historical CN interactive Pi UI flow passed 8/8 with lc_run Accepted, operation recovery Accepted, revision-bound Notes write, and lc_submit Accepted. Preserved evidence SHA-256 f82937d8... stores no credentials, raw code or Notes text, raw slug, or raw operation IDs. This remains cross-version remote-behavior evidence only: no live Notes write or lc_submit was repeated with 0.1.4. The exact 0.1.4 candidate independently passed the complete automated write-adapter, confirmation, duplicate/recovery, cancellation, credential-redaction, packed-install, and Pi activation suites.

invalidationConditions: the independent package-lock, source closure, release scripts or packed files change
the contract tuple, upstream mapping or exact artifact bytes change

requiredEvidence.evidence: Immutable annotated tag pi-leetcode-tools-v0.1.4 identifies release commit d72f60e741246d918c9f866c905815befcc62390, which is an ancestor of main. Main CI run 29713477233, tag CI run 29713609886 and release dry-run 29713783088 passed. Active ruleset 19161906 protects refs/tags/pi-leetcode-tools-v* against deletion and non-fast-forward updates.

https://www.npmjs.com/package/pi-leetcode-tools/v/0.1.4 exposes the exact approved 323586-byte / 162-file tgz. SHA-256 is b0b01ce931d692db960ac6626687288c999a3f7de81599488bff4df4440d9f7e, npm integrity is sha512-6KqtsUZ15YGYMHKbSPxF1udHuwjMGmc7WRk5N9LlhpzzTHRbUNB3BeAjcJxBuv6gRoLIkyWgh/tjz0B2gUjbag==, and shasum is e667adc1be4a00105435b36ab42b2a374a867834. The public npm packument currently reports latest=0.1.4 and next=0.1.4, with time.modified=2026-07-20T03:43:50.517Z. This current dist-tag state does not rewrite the immutable publication-completion evidence attached to the 0.1.4 GitHub Release.

OIDC release run 29713903406 published the exact tgz through release-tools.yml and npm SLSA provenance binds refs/tags/pi-leetcode-tools-v0.1.4, commit d72f60e741246d918c9f866c905815befcc62390 and the registry subject SHA-512. Attestation digest is sha256:ffd515fafdbe6de651879e927980474eda461e19640639890f3c26deffcf1b37; npm audit signatures passed with one signature and zero invalid or missing entries.

Release run 29713903406 re-downloaded byte-identical registry content, verified signature and provenance, performed a lifecycle-disabled clean install, matched all 162 installed files to CandidateRecord content digest sha256:dd945209f96cfcb6014a39ea9160cabb5cd99b1bfcf025db881976d22dedd765, activated 14/14 Pi tools through a closed allowlist registry, passed production audit and secret scan, and proved that at publication completion next=0.1.4 while latest remained 0.1.3. That result is the historical publication-instant invariant, not the current registry state after promotion.

After the exact 0.1.4 registry artifact, provenance, clean install and GitHub Release assets were verified, an npm owner-authorized manual `npm dist-tag add pi-leetcode-tools@0.1.4 latest` promotion moved latest from 0.1.3 to 0.1.4 without changing the immutable version bytes. A subsequent credential-free public registry read reports latest=0.1.4 and next=0.1.4; the packument modification time is 2026-07-20T03:43:50.517Z.

npm trust relationship 14250bf8-aa81-4b65-9c1b-015f716a7c5f is type github, repository Li7777777/pi-leetcode-tools, file release-tools.yml, environment npm-tools-next, with createPackage permission. The committed regular release workflow remains OIDC-only and rejects NPM_TOKEN.

The forward schema-2 regular release policy uses the publish-latest mode for stable versions that are strictly newer than the existing npm latest. The protected OIDC job publishes the one exact approved tgz directly with --tag latest, preserves every non-target dist-tag including next, and the credential-free verifier records the latest transition. The manual 0.1.4 promotion is historical remediation and is not the steady-state path for future regular releases.

GitHub environment secret NPM_TOKEN was deleted, bootstrap-tools.yml was disabled after the one-time publication, and temporary npm tokens leet and leet2fa were revoked. The final leet2fa whoami probe returned unauthorized after revocation.

GitHub Release https://github.com/Li7777777/pi-leetcode-tools/releases/tag/pi-leetcode-tools-v0.1.4 publishes the exact tgz together with SHA256SUMS, CycloneDX SBOM, supply-chain evidence, formal registry verification, dist-tag evidence and Pi activation evidence. All seven uploaded asset digests match the locally verified release set. The attached pi-leetcode-tools-0.1.4-dist-tag-evidence.json intentionally records the publication-completion state next=0.1.4 and latest=0.1.3; it remains immutable historical evidence and must not be interpreted as the current post-promotion registry state.

attemptHistory.reason: npm interpreted bootstrap-bundle/pi-leetcode-tools-0.1.2.tgz as a Git locator because the protected publish input lacked an explicit ./ local path prefix
The first 0.1.3 bootstrap secret write included a trailing newline, so npm rejected an illegal HTTP header after emitting provenance at transparency-log index 2202522560
Windows PowerShell Process.StandardInput.Write prefixed the replacement secret with UTF-8 BOM EFBBBF, so npm again rejected an illegal HTTP header after provenance index 2202577511

The protected publish job succeeded and emitted provenance index 2202596094. The original post-publish job failed because direct Node invocation omitted npm_execpath; the corrected npm-script verifier subsequently passed exact bytes, provenance, clean install, signature audit, Pi activation and initial dist-tags.

The protected OIDC publish-next job published the exact committed tgz, and the credential-free verifier passed registry bytes, SLSA provenance, signature audit, lifecycle-disabled clean install, Pi activation, supply-chain checks and the publication-completion latest dist-tag invariant. The later owner-authorized manual latest promotion is recorded separately by TOOLS-REL-MANUAL-LATEST-PROMOTION.

invalidationConditions: pi-leetcode-tools@0.1.4 is deprecated, unpublished or its current dist-tags move away from the required next=0.1.4 and latest=0.1.4 state
the immutable source tag, CandidateRecord, registry bytes, npm provenance or GitHub Release assets no longer agree
the npm Trusted Publisher or protected npm-tools-next environment is removed or broadened
the forward regular workflow no longer publishes an exact stable version directly to latest while preserving every non-target dist-tag
